interest: The interest behind this argument is that of equity advocates and disadvantaged/Roma pupils who want the sorting mechanism itself dismantled rather than its downstream effects merely funded.
value: Equity-as-access (ending early selection) is in tension with pragmatic harm-mitigation and political feasibility.
fear: The real fear is that a fundable, politically easy 'equity solution' will be adopted as the endpoint, locking in early selection permanently while giving cover to segregation and leaving a generation tracked into better-funded but still segregated schools.
affected: talented children in segregated primary schools
middle-class families invested in selective tracks
assumption: The claim rests on the unstated assumption that compensation cannot genuinely offset early-sorting effects and that adopting S4 forecloses rather than enables later structural reform.
empirical_uncertainty: The factual core is contested-to-unknown: the scenario itself flags 'can compensation offset rather than merely mask?' at [confidence: low], no comparable early-selection system has demonstrated offsetting at Hungary's age-10 selection, and the durability/fiscal-cut risk is tagged [evidence: weak — inferred].
